column3:
Each Santa Fe student can participate in up to 3 sports in Junior High and up to 4 in High School. The sports are as follows: baseball, basketball, football, softball, track & field, and volleyball. 

How many of these have you participated in for a Santa Fe school team? (Please do not count non-SF teams.)

column31:
1. Comparison to other teams- each year you have a different team and a whole new set of girls with new talents and abilities. I think it is important to understand what past teams are capable of and see what players had done in the past, but I don't believe it should almost be held over their head as to what other teams had done compared to the current team. 
2.  Communication- communicated with all players in a way it best fit the athlete. Not all athletes can be communicated with in the same way. Some people are more sensitive to communication and need to be talked to in a different way for them to understand and not get more "red" than they already are. 
3. Shutdown- When things don't go the way we want them to it's not ideal, but I think that we should still talk as a team. At the end of the day the athletes and coaches are a team and  when we didn't talk the environment was just pissed off and negative, this then carried over into the next day and possibly even practice. Even if they didn't have much to say or anything good to say, I believe we should still talk as a team. 
4. Build the team for the future- We have a very past driven mindset and like to succeed and win. I think we like to have the best team possible for that year, which is nice because we can win but I feel as though it makes the next year harder and more intimidating for the new "Staters" because there is a chance they have never been in a position like that and they are jumping in head first. Even if they are an upperclassmen and now have a role on the court/field that they hadn't had before it is different when playing competition then in practice.
column32:
1. Communication- communicated with all players in a way it best fit the athlete. Not all athletes can be communicated with in the same way. Some people are more sensitive to communication and need to be talked to in a different way for them to understand and not get more "red" than they already are. 
2. Constant Communication- They talked all the time even when our team was down. Talking players through tough times can sometimes help them understand they are capable of getting the next one and how to do it. 
3. Leader- If they expected something out of us as players they did it themselves. 
4. Explanations- Some people need more in depth explanations for drills or skills. Our coaches generally do a good job of making sure the athletes understand what they are supposed to do or how to do it. 
5. Resolving Conflict- I think all of our coaches handle conflict pretty well and try to resolve it before it gets too bad. 
6. Fair- All of our coaches treat players fairly and do what they believe is best for the team when making decisions.
column33: Active Listener- listen to their team's ideas and try to see if it is a common idea. If it is try and find a way to make it fit into a time to talk about it or do it. Supportive- Support my goals on their court but also on other teams and in life. Knowledge- Have a good understanding of the sport and different coaching styles. Challenging- Willing to push me to be the best I can be.
